jobs.title: Director of Development
jobs.description:
**About First Place AZ**

We are a spirited charitable nonprofit organization advancing a new marketplace of inclusive housing, healthcare, employment and lifelong\-learning options for adults with autism, neurodivergences and/or intellectual/developmental disabilities (I/DD). First Place\-Phoenix, our flagship property, serves as a proof point informing our national work, offering a real\-world site for innovation, learning and demonstration.

First Place AZ is an equal opportunity employer.

**The Opportunity**

Philanthropy at First Place is relational, strategic and mission driven. We don’t just raise money—we build lasting relationships with individuals, foundations and corporations who share our vision and want to be part of something that’s working. Our donors are allies in a movement to reimagine what’s possible for adults with disabilities.

This role is for someone who wants to shape a development function at a pivotal moment. You’ll work closely with the CEO and Board to set philanthropic strategy, diversify and grow revenue, and strengthen the systems that support it all. You’ll be a member of the leadership team, not a siloed fundraiser.

We’re looking for ambition, maturity and aspiration. Someone who sees what First Place is building and is committed to taking it further.

**Who You Are**

You are a seasoned development professional who has outgrown transactional fundraising. You know how to secure major gifts, write winning proposals and steward donors—and you are hungry to do that work in service of a mission you believe in and an organization on the rise.

You think strategically about revenue, not just tactically about asks. You value the entire development ecosystem: individual giving, foundations, corporate partnerships, planned giving, events—and you know how to build a diversified, sustainable funding base.

You are a relationship builder and are as comfortable in a one\-on\-one donor or team meeting as you are presenting to a board. You bring people along.

You lead with clarity and accountability. You have supervised and mentored staff, managed budgets and built systems that work. You communicate performance honestly and use data to guide decisions.

You are ready to be part of a leadership team, assume a seat at the table where strategy is shaped and contribute beyond your functional area.

**What You’ll Do**

**Strategic Leadership**

You will develop and execute First Place’s philanthropic strategy in partnership with the CEO, Board of Directors and leadership team. This includes setting annual fundraising goals and long\-term revenue targets, identifying opportunities to diversify funding streams, preparing development budgets and projections, and monitoring trends to keep the organization ahead of the curve. You will establish policies, procedures and practices that strengthen department efficiency and position the organization for continued growth.

**Revenue Development**

You will lead fundraising across all channels—individual giving, foundations, corporations and planned giving.

For individual donors, you will build and manage a comprehensive giving program, develop annual campaigns and stewardship strategies, identify and cultivate major gift prospects, expand recurring giving , and support the CEO’s donor portfolio.

For foundations and grants, you will oversee the full cycle from prospect research through submission and reporting, develop compelling proposals and budgets, manage funder relationships, and maintain compliance calendars and pipelines.

For corporate collaborators, you will cultivate long\-term partnerships, secure sponsorships for the annual events and initiatives, and ensure fulfillment of sponsorship benefits.

For planned giving, you will lead the growth of the First Place Forever legacy program, recruit, support the Planned Giving Advisory Committee and develop donor engagement opportunities.

**Engagement \& Events**

You will provide strategic leadership for fundraising and cultivation events, including the annual Dimensions® symposium, community tours, donor events, educational webinars and special initiatives. You will recruit and engage volunteers, support Board committees and advisory groups, and coordinate volunteer communications. In partnership with communications and marketing staff, you will shape fundraising campaigns, impact reports, donor communications, annual appeals, sponsorship materials, case statements and website content.

**Operations \& Team Leadership**

You will oversee development operations, including Salesforce database integrity, dashboards and reporting, fundraising KPIs, gift acknowledgment processes and coordination with the finance department. You will supervise development staff, interns and volunteers, providing coaching, mentoring and professional development. You will foster a collaborative, mission\-driven team culture.

**Supervisory Responsibilities**

This role supervises both full\-time and part\-time Development team members, interns and volunteers.

**What You’ll Bring**

* Bachelor’s degree or equivalent professional experience required.
* Certified Fund Raising Executive (CFRE) or interest in obtaining certification *preferred*.
* Eight or more years of progressive nonprofit fundraising experience.
* Experience with fundraising for disability, education or human services organizations *preferred*.
* Demonstrated success securing major gifts, grants and corporate sponsorships.
* Experience managing fundraising databases, preferably Salesforce.
* Experience with nonprofit budgeting and financial reporting *preferred*.
* Exceptional written, verbal and presentation skills.
* Strong project management and organizational abilities.
* Experience developing fundraising strategy and annual work plans.
* Ability to analyze fundraising data and communicate performance metrics.
* Experience supervising staff or leading cross\-functional teams.
* Experience planning conferences or large fundraising

**About Bedrock Talent**
------------------------


At Bedrock Talent, we're intentional about the companies we represent.


We don't partner with every startup that comes our way. We work with founders we'd be excited to work for ourselves, companies solving meaningful problems, and teams we genuinely believe have the potential to build category\-defining businesses.


This is one of those companies.

**About the Company**
---------------------


Our client is an early\-stage healthcare AI company with an ambitious mission: leveraging artificial intelligence, data science, and clinical expertise to improve health outcomes and meaningfully extend healthy human lives.


Backed by an exceptional founding team and world\-class advisors spanning healthcare, technology, AI, and longevity science, the company is building a platform designed to transform complex health data into actionable insights for patients, providers, and researchers.


They're still in the earliest stages of company building, which means this role offers a rare opportunity to shape not only the business, but the future direction of an emerging category.

**The Opportunity**
-------------------


We're looking for a **Chief Executive Officer** who wants to build more than a company—they want to build a legacy.


This is a true 0 1 leadership opportunity for an operator who thrives at the intersection of product, commercialization, fundraising, and company building.


You'll partner with an experienced executive team and renowned advisors to define product\-market fit, establish go\-to\-market strategy, raise capital, recruit exceptional talent, and guide the company from its earliest stages through Series A and beyond.


This role is ideal for someone who enjoys rolling up their sleeves, making decisions with imperfect information, and building organizations from first principles.

What it needs is a CEO who can connect product to market, vision to execution, and innovation to commercial success.


You'll help define nearly every aspect of how the company grows—from fundraising and hiring to customer acquisition and product strategy.

This isn't a "fundraising CEO" or a "sales CEO."


The ideal leader understands customers deeply, helps shape product strategy, builds repeatable go\-to\-market motions, and translates technical innovation into real customer value.

### **Mission Comes First**


The company exists to tackle one of humanity's biggest challenges: helping people live healthier, longer lives.


The team is looking for someone who believes meaningful businesses can generate exceptional returns while creating measurable improvements in people's lives.

### **Exceptional Founders and Advisors**


One of the reasons Bedrock Talent chose to partner with this company is the people behind it.


The founders and advisors are thoughtful, ambitious, low ego, and deeply committed to building the right company—not simply the fastest\-growing one.


They're looking for a CEO who shares those values.

------------------

* Develop and own the company's go\-to\-market strategy, positioning, and pricing.
* Lead fundraising efforts from pre\-seed through Series A.

* Represent the company with customers, investors, partners, and the broader healthcare and AI ecosystem.

Department Website Link Business Affairs
  
Location Tucson Campus
  
Address Tucson, AZ USA
  
Position Highlights The University of Arizona seeks a strategic and experienced leader to serve as its Executive Director of the Academic Financial Office. In this pivotal role, you work in close collaboration with the Chief Financial Officer, the Chief Budget \& Fiscal Management Officer, the Chief Human Resources Officer (CHRO), the Provost, and the Deans, providing financial oversight for all 20 colleges of the University. Your responsibilities directly support the University's core academic mission, initiatives focused on student success, academic affairs, faculty and staff excellence, research, and key strategic objectives.
  
  

Reporting to the Chief Financial Officer (CFO), you will be a vital member of both the CFO and the Provost's administrative leadership teams. Your expertise will be instrumental in developing and implementing financial strategies for the near and long\-term, directly contributing to the University's overall academic and strategic achievements. Success in this role requires a comprehensive understanding of finance, outstanding management capabilities, and a versatile professional background relevant to the nuanced financial landscape of an R1 university's central academic administration.
  
  

As the Executive Director of the Academic Financial Office, you will have significant autonomy in designing and implementing a new model for financial operations of the colleges, which will be aligned with the University’s new budget model (Arizona Forward), eventually building and leading a team of finance professionals. You will foster a collaborative and customer\-focused environment while ensuring financial goals and daily operations are aligned with CFO and Provost's vision for academic excellence and student success.
  
  

Visa sponsorship is not available for this position.
  
  

Outstanding U of A benefits include health, dental, and vision insurance plans; life insurance and disability programs; paid vacation, sick leave, and holidays; U of A/ASU/NAU tuition reduction for the employee and qualified family members; retirement plans; access to U of A recreation and cultural activities; and more!
  
  

The University of Arizona has been recognized for our innovative work\-life programs.
  
  

Duties \& Responsibilities
  
  

**Academic Financial Team Leadership:**  

Lead the transition from a distributed academic financial model to a new structure that is aligned with the university’s financial structures and strategic imperatives and is managed in the CFO’s office in collaboration with academic leadership.
  
Exercise direct, solid\-line supervisory authority over and performance management of approximately 20 leaders/managers, fostering a culture of accountability, professional growth, and "whole\-university" thinking that transcends individual unit interests.
  
Lead and inspire a distributed workforce of over 200 business and finance professionals by establishing clear standards for operational excellence, transparency, and ethical stewardship across all academic units.
  
Set the cultural tone, model the collaborative behavior needed, and align the technical functions of academic business administration with the university's academic and research mission, ensuring all team members understand how their fiscal roles directly support faculty and staff success and student outcomes.
  
Drive large\-scale cultural change by implementing consistent, equitable practices in hiring, workload distribution, and professional development across the academic portfolio, reducing "silo" behaviors and promoting cross\-unit collaboration.
  
With the support of the Office of Business Affairs and with University Communications, design and execute a comprehensive internal communication strategy \- including "all\-hands" interactions, functional working groups, and digital tool sets \- to ensure all members of the team remain informed, engaged, and aligned with the CFO and Provost's strategic priorities.
  
Continuously assess and adjust leadership roles and team structures to ensure optimal efficiency and effectiveness in achieving the financial and University\-wide goals.
  
  

**Strategic Financial Planning \& Execution:**  

Lead the development of a comprehensive suite of multi\-year financial models for the colleges supporting initiatives related to areas such as academic programs, faculty development, student success, optimization of academic revenue streams and the fiscal impact of enrollment fluctuations. Take a lead role in the change management necessary for success.
  
Partner with the Provost, CHRO, and Deans to model and manage the long\-term fiscal impact of faculty affairs, including the competitive structuring of start\-up packages, retention funding, tenure\-track line allocations, and instructional capacity planning across all 20 colleges.
  
Oversee the execution of innovative operational plans and systems for integrated financial operations and budgeting processes strategically aligning the administration of academics with the University's strategic goals.
  
Facilitate cross\-functional collaboration across the colleges and with other central administrative units to integrate financial efforts related to the CFO's responsibilities.
  
Proactively identify and fix the root causes of financial challenges and opportunities related to academic strategic priorities.
  
  

**Financial Evaluation \& Business Planning:**  

Oversee the preparation of business and operational plans, ensuring a comprehensive evaluation of financial viability and strategic alignment.
  
Develop a standardized process for and conduct comprehensive financial due diligence for new programs and initiatives, ensuring a thorough examination of financial considerations.
  
Drive continuous improvement initiatives in financia

**EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR**

**Job Summary**
===============


The Executive Director is responsible for the overall leadership, growth, and profitability of an assigned mortgage lending brand operating within GenWay’s portfolio of Wholesale and/or Correspondent business. Acting as the senior leader and primary ambassador of the brand, this role is accountable for driving sales production, broker acquisition and retention, market expansion and financial performance while aligning business activities with corporate objectives and operational standards. **Job Duties****Leadership \& Business Management*** Lead the overall business strategy, growth initiatives, and performance of an assigned lending brand.
* Maintain accountability for the brand's production volume, revenue generation, profitability, and achievement of established business objectives.
* Develop and execute annual business plans, sales strategies, and growth initiatives designed to increase market share and brand presence.
* Analyze production, financial, and operational performance metrics to identify trends, opportunities, and areas requiring corrective action.
* Provide regular business updates, forecasts, and strategic recommendations to the Chief Executive Officer and Executive Leadership team.
* Manage the brand's profit and loss performance, including revenue growth, expense management, and overall financial results.

**Sales Leadership \& Talent Development*** Recruit, hire, develop, and retain top\-performing sales professionals in partnership with corporate leadership.
* Lead, coach, and mentor Account Executives, Account Managers, and other team members associated with the brand.

**Cross\-Functional Collaboration*** Partner with Operations, Underwriting, Capital Markets, Compliance, Secondary Marketing, and other corporate departments to ensure an exceptional broker and customer experience.
* Advocate for the needs of brokers and sales teams while supporting enterprise\-wide objectives and organizational initiatives.
* Collaborate with corporate leadership on organizational planning, staffing strategies, and business expansion efforts.
* Support the development and implementation of policies, procedures, and best practices that enhance operational efficiency and scalability.
* Oversee operational support personnel assigned to the brand, as applicable.

* **Creativity, Innovation \& Critical Thinking –** Evaluates situations from multiple angles to identify root causes and generate innovative, effective solutions that align with goals.
* **Customer Service –** Understands and maintains client needs and relationships, internal and external, as the primary driver of work goals and activities.
* **Positive Attitude –** Promotes a constructive and solution\-orientated work environment through a consistently positive demeanor.
 **Supervisory Responsibilities*** Provides leadership, direction, coaching, and performance management to assigned team members, including sales and operational personnel as applicable.
* Participates in recruiting, interviewing, hiring, onboarding, and talent development efforts in partnership with Corporate leadership and Human Resources.
* Establishes performance expectations, monitors productivity and pipeline performance, and holds team members accountable for achieving individual and team goals.
* Conducts ongoing coaching, feedback, and performance discussions to support employee development, engagement, and retention.
* Delegates responsibilities, prioritizes workloads, and allocates resources to support business objectives and operational efficiency.
* Promotes a culture of accountability, collaboration, professionalism, and continuous improvement while addressing performance concerns in accordance with Company policies.

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
Under general supervision of the Chief Executive Officer, the Chief Operating Officer (“COO”) has a crucial role in driving operational excellence, ensuring the delivery of high\-quality healthcare services, and advancing WIHCC’s mission to provide accessible and compassionate care to the communities that WIHCC, Inc. serves. The COO is responsible for overseeing the day\-to\-day operations, implementing strategic initiatives, and fostering collaboration among various departments to achieve organizational goals. Directs strategy development, planning, and administrative management of WIHCC, Inc. Establishes and maintains standards focusing on effective translation of strategy into operational objectives and specific, measurable goals and associated deliverables consistent with practice standards and requirements and which align/support overall mission, vision and values of WIHCC, Inc. Serves on the Executive Team. Maintains confidentiality of all privileged and sensitive information. Upholds the principles of WIHCC’s Vision, Mission, and Value Statements. Maintains confidentiality of all privileged information at all times.

*This list of duties and responsibilities is illustrative only of the tasks performed by this position and is not all\-inclusive.*




**Essential Duties \& Responsibilities:**

* Maintains regular attendance and punctuality.
* Collaborates with the CEO and other executive leaders to develop and execute strategic plans into actionable steps for growth and implementation that aligns with the WIHCC, Inc.’s mission and objectives.
* Provides guidance on operational initiatives to enhance efficiency, quality of care, and overall organizational effectiveness.
* Oversees WIHCC’s Inc. operations and employee productivity, building a highly inclusive culture that ensures team members can thrive and the organizational goals are met. ;
* Implements and monitors operational policies, procedures, standards of operations, and protocols to maintain compliance with industry standards and regulations.
* Integrates departments and services ensuring quality, accessible, and safe health care environment for patients and staff.
* Provides strategic leadership and oversight across all clinical and non\-clinical operations by identifying organizational priorities, advising executive leadership on strategic issues and opportunities, planning, implementing, and evaluating organizational goals, policies, and performance. Aligns operations with the organization's mission and strategic priorities while ensuring operational excellence, regulatory compliance, fiscal stewardship, quality outcomes, and the effective management of programs, functions, services, activities, and resources.
* Addresses performance concerns promptly by implementing appropriate corrective actions while ensuring compliance with personnel policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.
* Assures that the work environment for all supervised employees is fair, equitable and free of discrimination or harassment and practices and encourages exemplary customer services and cultural sensitivity.
* Provides executive leadership and operational planning with the CFO, CEO, ET, and BOD to develop, implement, monitor, and evaluate organizational budgets, financial forecasts, and resource allocation strategies that support strategic priorities and long\-term sustainability.
* Identifies and implements strategies to enhance organizational financial sustainability through cost optimization, revenue growth, and resource development initiatives while maintaining a strong commitment to high\-quality patient care and mission\-driven outcomes.
* Collaborates with Indian Health Service, P.L.93\-638 organizations, tribal health departments, federal, states, counties and community health and social service agencies to achieve goals and objectives of WIHCC, Inc.; orients and trains staff of such agencies as required.
* Works with CEO to meet goals and objectives established under WIHCC, Inc.’s Strategic Plan and develop, implement criteria/measurable standards to monitor and improve programs, organizational performance and provides consultative suggestions/advisements in line with Joint Commission (JC); to include Critical Access Hospital (CAH), and Centers for Medicare \& Medicaid Services (CMS) references.
* Prepares and presents various reports to Executive Team, Board of Directors, 638 Association and Navajo Nation Oversight Committee, (e.g. HEHSC, RDC, etc.).
* Adheres to JC elements of performance, HIPPA and Privacy Act and organization policy and procedures.
* Requires completion of tasks or duties assigned by a supervisor.
* Upholds all principles of confidentiality and patient care to the fullest extent.
* Adheres to all professional and ethical behavior standards of the healthcare industry.
* Adheres to WIHCC, Inc.’s Personnel Policies and Procedures, departmental policies, rules, and regulations.
* Interacts in an honest, trustworthy and dependable manner with patients, employees, visitors, and vendors.
* Possesses cultural awareness and sensitivity.
* Maintains compliance with all Human Resources requirements.
* Performs other duties as assigned.

**Minimum Qualifications:**


Master’s degree in Business Administration, Health Care Administration, Public Administration, or closely related field. Five or more years of progressive executive leadership experience in a healthcare administration with an executive leadership role including responsibilities for development and implementation of strategic programs, functions, services, activities, policies and practices. In addition, in support of understanding of advanced business planning and regulatory issues covering overall operational mission, goals and objectives. Proven success in leading and implementing strategic initiatives within a healthcare setting.


Bilingual skills in English and Navajo language preferred. Must maintain a valid unrestricted and insurable driver’s license.

jobs.title: Assistant Director of Institutional Giving
jobs.description:
**Assistant Director of Individual Giving**

**Reports to:** Director of Development

 **Functional Area:** Advancement Department

 **FLSA Status:** Exempt

 **Job Location:** Ward 5

 **Employment Type:** Full\-Time

 **Classification:** Manager

 **Direct Reports:** 1 (Individual Giving Specialist)

  



  

**POSITION OVERVIEW**


The Assistant Director of Individual Giving plays a central role in growing and diversifying Academy of Hope's (AoH) revenue base by building a strong, sustainable individual giving program. This role is especially important as AoH strengthens the day\-to\-day systems that support consistent donor engagement and stewardship.


Reporting to the Director of Development, the Assistant Director of Individual Giving will develop and implement AoH's individual giving strategy and annual plan — in partnership with, and subject to approval by, the Director of Development — manage performance against goals, and maintain a disciplined moves\-management and stewardship cadence in Salesforce CRM.


The Assistant Director will directly supervise and coach the Individual Giving Specialist, oversee the Annual Fund strategy and results, and oversee donor engagement event logistics.


This role also partners closely with the Director of Development and Chief Growth Officer — providing donor data, portfolio analysis, and prospect research that inform prioritization decisions and help prepare AoH leadership for high\-impact donor engagements. The ideal candidate brings an entrepreneurial mindset: someone who can build systems and processes that don't yet exist, test and refine approaches, and help AoH grow individual giving in a way that is measurable, repeatable, and sustainable.

**A. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS AND RESPONSIBILITIES**

**Individual Giving Strategy, Pipeline Growth \& Moves Management** *(35%)*

* Develop and implement AoH's Individual Giving Plan (annual fund through leadership\-level gifts), in partnership with and subject to approval by the Director of Development, aligned with AoH's strategic priorities and revenue\-diversification goals.
* Establish and maintain a practical moves\-management cadence (e.g., weekly next\-step review, monthly portfolio refresh, quarterly pipeline health check) that drives measurable movement and reduces stalled relationships.
* Partner with the Director of Development to identify, qualify, and cultivate high\-capacity prospects, and support portfolio strategy and donor\-movement decisions.
* Track performance and pipeline health using Salesforce dashboards and regular reporting (pipeline value, donor movement, retention, attrition, upgrades, solicitations, and outcomes by segment).
* Maintain consistent processes and data standards in Salesforce so individual giving data is reliable for forecasting, decision\-making, and leadership reporting.
* Manage a personal portfolio of donors and prospects with giving potential up to $25,000, using clear moves management (stages, next steps, timelines) to advance relationships over time.
* Drive AoH's leadership annual giving program (the tier just below major gifts), including segmentation, cultivation, stewardship, and annual targets, in coordination with the Director of Development.
* Participate directly in solicitations for the leadership annual giving and mid\-level tiers, including strategy prep, donor meetings, proposal\-drafting support, follow\-up, and documenting outcomes in Salesforce within agreed\-upon timelines.

**Mid\-Level Giving \& Donor Relationship Management** *(20%)*

* Oversee a portfolio of mid\-level donors ($1,000–$9,999\), partnering with the Individual Giving Specialist on day\-to\-day cultivation and stewardship execution while retaining accountability for strategy, segmentation, and upgrade identification.
* Support the Director of Development in preparing senior leadership and board members for donor engagement and solicitation by developing donor and prospect briefing materials tailored to the meeting purpose (cultivation, stewardship, renewal, upgrade, or ask).
* Build and maintain meeting\-prep toolkits for donor visits (contact history, giving/engagement summary, affinity and interests, proposed talking points, "the ask," roles in the meeting, and next steps).
* In partnership with the Director of Development, develop board toolkits to strengthen annual fund execution and donor stewardship (call scripts, email templates, segmented call lists, talking points, and tracking instructions).
* Ensure donor experiences are consistent, personal, and aligned with AoH's mission and culture.

**Annual Fund Oversight** *(20%)*

* Oversee and manage the Annual Fund program, including strategy, calendar, segmentation, and performance tracking across direct mail, email, digital, and stewardship.
* Ensure campaign execution is consistent and measurable (e.g., Giving Tuesday, end\-of\-year, back\-to\-school, and spring campaign).
* Draft and coordinate annual fund messaging and stewardship communications to ensure alignment, tone, and outcomes\-based storytelling, with final approval from the Director of Development.

**Donor Engagement Events \& Cultivation Moments** *(10%)*

* Oversee donor engagement opportunities that strengthen relationships and move donors toward deeper investment (small cultivation gatherings, site visits, graduation touchpoints, and tailored experiences).
* Partner with internal teams to build authentic engagement moments tied to AoH programs and strategic priorities.
* Ensure every event has a defined fundraising purpose (stewardship, upgrade, prospecting, or cultivation) and a clear follow\-up plan in Salesforce (attendance logged, portfolio routed, next step set within a defined timeframe).

**Management, Cross\-Team Coordination \& Budget Oversight** *(15%)*

* Supervise, coach, and support the Individual Giving Specialist — setting clear priorities, conducting regular one\-on\-ones and an annual performance review, and supporting their profes

jobs.title: Senior Director of Development - Lewis Katz School of Medicine
jobs.description:
**Temple University's Lewis Katz School of Medicine is searching for a Sr. Director of Development!**

**Salary Range: $125,000\-$135,000**  

A variety of important factors are reviewed by HR when considering salary, including job duties, the applicant’s education and experience, all relevant internal equity considerations, department budget, and funding source.


Become a part of the Temple family and you will have access to the following:  

**Full medical, dental, vision coverage**  

**Paid time off**  

**9 Federally Observed Paid Holidays**  

**3 Personal Days**  

**Tuition remission \- eligible employees and their dependents can obtain a degree TUITION FREE**  

**A generous retirement plan and so much more!**

**Position Summary:**  

The Temple Health Sciences Campus is seeking an entrepreneurial, motivated and results\-driven professional with proven experience in cultivating, securing and stewarding major and principal level gifts to join our high\-achieving team as Sr. Director of Development (SDOD), Lewis Katz School of Medicine (School) at Temple University and working in conjunction with (Podiatry, Pharmacy and Dentistry) to help fundraise towards Temple University’s upcoming capital campaign. This is an exceptional opportunity for an accomplished major/principal gifts officer to lead and manage a team of frontline fundraisers and partner with School leadership, advancement colleagues and stakeholders across the School, University and Temple University Hospital (TUH) to fuel medical education, scientific research, patient care and service to the community with impactful philanthropy. Come join a vibrant world\-class academic R1 research institution on the ground floor of what will be a historic comprehensive campaign.


The SDOD must be a highly energetic, driven, organized, intellectually curious and enthusiastic individual with a track record of building relationships, establishing trust and facilitating meaningful donor\-centric giving opportunities and partnerships. The successful candidate will work collaboratively and strategically, demonstrate good judgment, and evidence a strong application of principles, ethics and best practices of successful fundraising in a complex organization.


Founded in 1901 as Pennsylvania’s first co\-educational medical school, the Lewis Katz School of Medicine has earned a national reputation for training outstanding clinicians and biomedical scientists, attracting students, faculty, and staff committed to improving lives. The School and TUH, its chief clinical training site, provide care for patients from the region and beyond seeking advanced tertiary\- and quaternary\-level care. TUH also serves the local community, one of the most vulnerable populations in the nation, providing more free and under\-reimbursed care than any other hospital in the state.


The Temple Health Sciences Campus strives to maintain a culture of diversity and inclusion, a collaborative team\-based culture that values innovation and creativity, and a commitment to leadership opportunities, professional development and a healthy work culture.

**Job Details:**  

* This position requires a background check for cash handling responsibilities.
**Required Education and Experience:**  

* Bachelor’s degree and at least 7 years of progressively responsible higher education and/or healthcare fundraising experience with a proven track record of closing six to seven figure gifts. An equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.
**Required Skills and Abilities:**  

* Proven experience and success in strategizing and leading the solicitation of major and principal level gifts
* Knowledge of, and experience with, both the art and science of major gift fundraising
* Demonstrated ability to raise and close major and principal level gifts (including current use, endowed and estate gifts)
* Demonstrated track record of fundraising success in a fast\-paced, large organizational structure
* Must possess the negotiation, interpersonal and networking skills necessary for building relationships with external and internal constituents
* Demonstrated success in collaborating with senior management, school leadership, clinicians, faculty and staff
* Proven ability to engage and interact effectively with diverse constituents and serve as a charismatic ambassador
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to express information, including funding needs and giving opportunities
* Proven ability to take initiative and work both independently and collaboratively, as appropriate
* Ability to make policy decisions when necessary, multi\-task, set priorities, adapt to changes, meet deadlines and achieve goals
* Experience in talent development and performance management
* Commitment to professional development, learning, being mentored and mentoring others
* Commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion
* Capability with Microsoft 365, as well as Banner, FPM (Blackbaud), or similar CRM/fundraising databases
* Ability to travel and work evening/weekend hours, as needed
* CFRE, PlusDelta, or other formal major gift fundraising training completed
* Experience in an academic and/or medical advancement setting
**Essential Duties:**  

* In collaboration and partnership with the AVP, advancement colleagues, Dean and School leadership, strategize and set fundraising priorities to aggressively increase philanthropic giving opportunities to achieve agreed\-upon goals and objectives for the Health Sciences Campus and campaign readiness
* Lead and manage donor and prospect activity for the School and Health Sciences Campus and translate current and emerging priorities into fundraising plans and goals
* Lead the creation and implementation of an annual major/principal fundraising and planned giving plan for the School and Health Sciences Campus, working closely with the alumni relations, communications, stewardship and events teams to meaningfully e

jobs.title: Deputy Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
**About Democratic National Committee** **We’re hiring! Are you fired up to elect Democrats up and down the ticket?****Position Summary:*** The DNC is seeking a highly motivated Deputy Chief Operating Officer (Deputy COO) to provide support to and partner with the Chief Operating Officer (COO) in the execution and management of all hands\-on operational aspects and day\-to\-day functions of the Committee. Critical for success at this point in the campaign cycle, this position will assist the COO in providing both leadership and operational HR expertise across the organization, as well as help manage expansion of processes throughout the midterm cycle and beyond for our work with coordinated campaign efforts.The Deputy COO will act as a thought partner and support in developing strategic initiatives that meet the needs of a fast paced organization and election cycle.

**Responsibilities:*** Assist the COO in providing overall operational leadership of the Committee in formulating and achieving objectives in areas such as human resources, staff and building security, travel, events, risk management, contracting, diversity equity and inclusion (“DEI”), state support for state coordinated campaigns, asset\-tracking;
* Collaborate with the COO on matters involving compliance and insurance activities;
* Provide counsel to the COO on significant matters affecting internal and external operations and policies, including major organizational events;
* Support the COO in spearheading the development, recommendation, implementation, and communication of new or revised systems, policies, and procedures;
* Collaborate with the COO in developing and implementing short\-range and long\-range goals and business plans, along with measures of effectiveness, ensuring proper internal controls and procedures;
* Represent the COO and the DNC, as designated, in meetings before DNC, community and business group meetings;
* In cooperation with the COO, build and maintain relationships with external partners and stakeholders, as appropriate;
* Provide direct support to and monitor activities of assigned departments to ensure conformance with goals and objectives of reporting unit and eliminate impediments to peak performance;
* Assist in the management of Operations Facility Management teams;
* Assist in the maintenance, support and promotion of a safe work environment while complying with all of the DNC’s safety rules, policies, and procedures; and

The DNC will not tolerate any unlawful discrimination and any such conduct is strictly prohibited. **Benefits:*** The DNC offers a generous benefit package, including:

jobs.description:
**POSITION ANNOUNCEMENT**

**New Orleans Recreation Development Commission**

**POSITION: Chief Executive Officer, New Orleans Recreation Development**

**COMMISSION FUNCTION:** The mission of the New Orleans Recreation Development Commission (NORDC) is to advance the physical, mental, and social well\-being of New Orleanians by providing safe and welcoming environments for recreational, athletic, and cultural experiences. The Commission plans, supervises, conducts, and coordinates programs for cultural and physical recreation that will be administered by the New Orleans Recreation Development Commission. NORDC provides a wide range of sports, health, aquatics, day care, and fitness programs for residents and visitors of all ages. NORDC manages senior services, therapeutic recreation, youth camps, teen development, and athletic programs in over 160 facilities city\-wide. The Commission promotes cooperative planning with public and private entities concerned with recreation.

**REQUIREMENTS:**

* Minimum five years of supervisory experience in the administration of a parks and recreation department or sports organization.
* Experience with facilities, operations, and leading and managing professional staff and teams to higher levels of performance.
* Bachelor’s degree or any equivalent combination of education, training, and experience.

**SALARY RANGE**: $150,000 to $200,000

**POSITION SUMMARY**

The CEO of the Commission will have the responsibility to plan, organize, and direct the employees and programs of NORDC; to oversee development, implementation, and administration of NORD policies, programs, and facilities; to manage daily operations; to ensure the effective maintenance and operations of recreation facilities, including all outdoor athletic and recreational centers; to coordinate with and delegate to outside agencies and community groups; to assist in building financial and strategic support for NORDC; and to provide highly effective and comprehensive information and support to the Commission. * Review reports submitted by staff members in order to recommend approval or to suggest changes.

*Community Functions —*

* Respond to and resolve difficult and sensitive citizen inquiries and complaints; review and reply to daily correspondence and phone inquiries.
* Build community support of and engagement with NORDC's mission and strategic plan.
* Develop relationships with individuals and organizations in an effort to financially and strategically support NORDC's mission and strategic plan.
* Represent NORDC to outside agencies and the community; coordinate assigned activities with outside agencies and organizations.

* National best practices of youth development programming

* Principles and practices of marketing and public relations. This includes marketing strategy and tactics, promotion of services provided, and assessments of current and future value of services provided by NORDC.

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
Delta Health is seeking a visionary, collaborative, and mission\-driven **Chief Executive Officer (CEO)** to lead our organization into its next chapter. This is an exceptional opportunity to make a meaningful impact in a rural community while guiding a financially patient\-centered healthcare organization committed to **Excellence, Every Patient, Every Time.**



Reporting directly to the Board of Directors, the CEO serves as the organization's senior executive leader and is responsible for strategic direction, operational excellence, financial stewardship, physician partnerships, workforce engagement, and community relationships.


**What You'll Do**



As CEO, you will:


* Lead the development and execution of the organization's strategic vision.
* Foster a culture focused on quality, safety, accountability, and exceptional patient care.
* Ensure the financial strength and long\-term sustainability of the organization.
* Build strong partnerships with physicians, employees, the Board of Directors, and community stakeholders.
* Champion operational excellence, innovation, and continuous improvement.
* Ensure compliance with all regulatory and accreditation requirements.
* Support leadership development while promoting an engaged and high\-performing workforce.
* Represent Delta Health throughout the community and strengthen regional healthcare partnerships.


**What We're Looking For**



The ideal candidate will bring:


* 7\-10\+ years of rural and independent healthcare leadership experience.
* Previous CEO, COO, or senior executive leadership experience in a hospital or health system preferred.
* Master's degree in Healthcare Administration (MHA), Business Administration (MBA), or related field required.
* Bachelor's degree in Healthcare Administration, Business, or related discipline.
* Demonstrated financial acumen with knowledge of healthcare reimbursement and hospital operations.
* Experience working collaboratively with governing boards, medical staff, and executive leadership.
* Outstanding communication, relationship\-building, and leadership skills.
* Strong strategic thinking, data\-driven decision making, and emotional intelligence.
* FACHE certification preferred.
* ***As a special district it is expected that the candidate will reside in Delta County.***


**Why Delta Health?**



At Delta Health, you'll find more than a leadership opportunity—you'll find a community.



Located on Colorado's beautiful Western Slope, Delta offers an exceptional quality of life with year\-round outdoor recreation, affordable living, outstanding schools, and easy access to Grand Mesa National Forest, Black Canyon of the Gunnison National Park, world\-class skiing, hiking, mountain biking, fishing, and hunting.



As our next CEO, you'll have the opportunity to:


* Shape the future of healthcare in a growing rural community.
* Lead an engaged executive team and dedicated workforce.
* Build lasting partnerships with physicians and community leaders.
* Drive innovation while preserving the personalized care that defines rural healthcare.
* Leave a lasting legacy for generations of patients and families.


**Join Our Team**



If you are an authentic leader with a passion for rural healthcare, strategic growth, operational excellence, and community engagement, we encourage you to apply.


**Delta Health is an Equal Opportunity Employer.** We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive workplace where every employee can thrive.


Equal Opportunity Employer  

This employer is required to notify all applicants of their rights pursuant to federal employment laws. For further information, please review the Know Your Rights (https://www.eeoc.gov/poster) notice from the Department of Labor.

*Student U is an education nonprofit rooted in Durham.* ***We are students, families, and educators cultivating joy and academic excellence in learning communities so all students thrive in Durham and beyond.***  


*We believe everyone deserves opportunities in their educational journey that enable them to reach their full potential. This is not yet the reality for every student. Structural racism, intergenerational poverty, and other injustices create significant barriers for first\-generation college\-bound students and entire communities across Durham and the U.S., including here in Durham, N.C.*  


*We support students and their families inside and outside of school, helping them overcome challenges to reach college graduation. At the same time, we work to improve the public education system by sharing successful strategies with schools and educators and building a strong community at The W.G. Pearson Center, aiming for a better future for our youth.**We show up for students and families with joy, rigor, and deep belief that every person is worthy of opportunities that help them grow and thrive.*
*That same culture extends to our team. We are a collaborative, growth\-minded staff that invests in each other the same way we invest in students. We celebrate wins, learn from challenges, and are committed to building an organization where everyone can do the best work of their career. If you are looking for a place where your skills will matter, your voice will be heard, and your work will directly change lives in Durham and across North Carolina, you have found it.*


**POSITION OVERVIEW**



Student U has grown from a $93,000 budget in 2007 to an estimated $6\.1M budget in 2026, and we are ready for the next chapter. We are building a robust individual major gifts program while continuing to deepen and diversify our relationships with foundations, government funders, and corporate partners. This is a rare opportunity to join a high\-performing advancement team at an inflection point and help shape what comes next.



The Development Director will serve as a key leader on the advancement team, reporting directly to the Chief Advancement Officer and working in close partnership with the Executive Director. This is a relationship\-centered, externally\-facing role for someone who is energized by connecting people to meaningful work and has the experience to translate those relationships into long\-term investment in our mission. The Development Director will own and lead Student U’s Major Gifts program, carrying a personal portfolio of donors at the 5\-figure gift level while also serving as a key partner to the Executive Director in managing relationships with our top 20 donors.

  


**JOB RESPONSIBILITIES**


**Individual Giving, Major Gifts, and Donor Stewardship (70%)**


* Build, lead, and continuously refine Student U’s Major Gifts program, serving as its primary architect and day\-to\-day driver
* Carry and grow a personal portfolio of major donors and prospects at the 5\-figure gift level, developing individualized engagement strategies to secure one\-time and multi\-year commitments
* Serve as a strategic partner to the Executive Director in managing relationships with Student U’s top 20 donors, providing preparation, follow\-through, and relationship continuity to support the Executive Director’s caseload
* Lead outreach, cultivation, and stewardship across your portfolio with warmth, intentionality, and consistent follow\-through
* Design and implement a meaningful stewardship program that keeps current donors connected, informed, and inspired by their impact on Student U students and families
* Ensure donors at every level feel genuinely valued through timely acknowledgment, personalized touchpoints, and regular impact updates
* Develop and implement a tiered recurring giving program to grow sustainable individual revenue
* Work closely with the Board of Directors to deepen board giving, strengthen board member engagement in fundraising, and build a culture of philanthropy at the governance level

  


**Foundations, Government, and Corporate Relationships (15%)**
* Support the growth of a portfolio of foundation, government, and corporate relationships, serving as a strategic thought partner
* Collaborate with the Director of Partnerships and the Grants Committee to ensure proposals are timely, accurate, compelling, and aligned with Student U's work and funder priorities
* Represent Student U at site visits, community events, and funder convenings

  


**Development Operations and Collaboration (15%)**
* Partner with the Data Manager to ensure the donor database (Salesforce) is maintained with accuracy and integrity, and that donor acknowledgment is prompt and meaningful
* Coordinate with the Chief Advancement Officer and Director of Finance on revenue forecasting and cash flow projections
* Bring a continuous improvement mindset to development systems, strategies, and processes, identifying opportunities to strengthen infrastructure and outcomes
* Serve as an engaged collaborator across teams, particularly with the program team, to maintain a clear and compelling picture of Student U's impact for donors

 **REQUIREMENTS \& KEY COMPETENCIES**



You are a seasoned development professional who leads with relationships and follows through with results. You know how to build authentic connections with donors and move them from curiosity to commitment, and you understand that keeping a donor is just as important as finding one. You are equally comfortable in a one\-on\-one donor conversation, on a stage presenting to a room, and in a spreadsheet tracking your pipeline.



You believe, as we do, that fundraising is an act of community\-building. You do not see donors as transactions. You see them as long\-term partners in a shared vision for Durham, and you take seriously the responsibility to keep them engaged and connected to the impact of their giving.

**MISISON STATEMENT**


The Fiesta® San Antonio Commission Inc. is a nonprofit, volunteer, self\-supporting organization that plans, promotes, coordinates, and preserves Fiesta® San Antonio through support of its Participating Member Organizations in presenting a safe, educational, annual celebration of the diverse heritage, culture and spirit of San Antonio for its residents and visitors.  



**WHAT IS FIESTA® SAN ANTONIO**


Fiesta® San Antonio started in 1891 as a one\-parade event to honor the memory of the heroes of the Alamo and the Battle of San Jacinto. That historic commemoration still takes place, but for more than a century, Fiesta® has grown into a celebration of San Antonio’s rich and diverse cultures and today is one of this nation’s premier festivals with an economic impact of more than $340 million.  



Each year, more than one hundred San Antonio Area Non\-Profit, Fiesta Member Organizations coordinate more than one hundred Official Fiesta® Events to raise funds to provide services to San Antonio citizens throughout the year.



The Fiesta San Antonio Commission’s purpose is to help each of its Member Organizations be as successful as they can be.



The Fiesta San Antonio Commission proudly supports Fiesta Member Organizations that support:


* Higher Education Scholarships and Programs
* Diverse Cultures
* Historical Significance / The Alamo
* Arts / Nature
* Military Programs
* Children’s Events / Programs
* Pets / Animal Welfare
* Inclusive Events / LGBTQ
* Sports / Health and Wellness
* Special Needs Community

**Organization Overview**

The original Freedom Quilting Bee (Bee), was a women’s quilting cooperative established in 1966 during the tumultuous times of the Civil Rights Movement. The cooperative manufactured quilts for the largest retailers in America; Sears, Bloomingdale’s and interior designers in New York City.

***Our Mission \- To teach, share and preserve threads of quilting arts, resistance \& legacy of Black women in Gee’s Bend and Alberta, Alabama.***

***Our Vision \- To inspire makers across the globe, sharing threads of transformative art, culture, and the pioneering legacy of Black women in Gee’s Bend and Alberta, Alabama.*** 

Freedom Quilting Bee Legacy (Legacy) was founded to ensure the heroic, historic, innovative, artistic and incredible odds\-beating work of the women of the original Bee isn’t lost to history. We celebrate and teach about the work and accomplishments of those women (and men) past and present who made the Bee such a powerful artistic and community force. We will offer real economic opportunities for a new generation of quilt makers and artisans.

We provide solutions to community needs, such as Internet access, adult learning and skills building.

We draw tourism to the area and offer a deeper experience of the history and current activities, with shops, lodging, classes and interactive exhibits.

We preserve the past and evolve into the future by creating jobs, building wealth and teaching life skills to improve our community and its people..

Our nonprofit organization is dedicated to making a positive impact on our community by providing outreach, access to history and tourism. We rely heavily on fundraising to sustain our programs and services, and we are seeking a Director of Development to lead our fundraising efforts.

**Job Summary**

The Director of Development is responsible for leading and executing the organization's revenue development strategy, with a focus on individual donations, corporate donations and sponsorships, foundation grants, and governmental grants. This role is ideal for a strong relationship builder, strategic communicator, and results\-oriented professional whose prior experience may come from business development, sales, account management, marketing, public relations, proposal writing, community engagement, or another transferable field.

While prior nonprofit or fundraising experience is helpful, it is not required. The successful candidate will bring a skillset that lends itself well to donor cultivation, partnership development, persuasive communication, opportunity identification, proposal development, and revenue goal achievement. This position will work closely with leadership, board members, staff, external partners, and funders to build sustainable financial support for the organization's mission and programs.

**Primary ResponsibilitiesFundraising Strategy and Revenue Development**

* Develop and implement an annual development plan that supports the organization's financial goals and long\-term sustainability.
* Identify, evaluate, and pursue opportunities for individual giving, corporate support, foundation funding, and government grants.
* Build and maintain a pipeline of prospects, active donors, funders, sponsors, and strategic partners.
* Track fundraising activity, revenue progress, deadlines, and follow\-up needs to ensure accountability and timely execution.

**Individual Donations**

* Cultivate and steward relationships with individual donors, including prospective donors, recurring donors, and major gift prospects.
* Create donor engagement strategies that communicate the organization's mission, impact, and funding needs in a clear and compelling way.
* Support donor outreach, appeals, acknowledgments, recognition efforts, and ongoing relationship management.

**Corporate Donations and Sponsorships**

* Develop relationships with businesses, corporate partners, and community stakeholders to secure donations, sponsorships, and partnership support.
* Create sponsorship materials, proposals, and partnership opportunities aligned with the organization's programs and visibility needs.
* Maintain strong communication with corporate partners to support retention, renewals, and expanded engagement.

**Foundation and Governmental Grants**

* Research foundation and governmental grant opportunities that align with the organization's mission, programs, and funding priorities.
* Coordinate the preparation and submission of grant proposals, applications, budgets, narratives, and supporting documentation.
* Work with leadership and program staff to gather accurate program information, outcomes, and financial details for proposals and reports.
* Monitor grant deadlines, reporting requirements, renewal opportunities, and compliance\-related follow\-up.

**Communications, Reporting, and Relationship Management**

* Prepare persuasive written materials, including donor communications, grant narratives, sponsorship proposals, impact summaries, presentations, and reports.
* Use data, outcomes, and stories to communicate the value and impact of the organization's work.
* Maintain accurate donor, funder, and partner records in the organization's CRM, donor database, or tracking system.

jobs.title: Club Executive Director - Dauch
jobs.description:
**Position Summary**



The Executive Director is responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for the Boys \& Girls Club. This role ensures the successful delivery of high\-quality youth development programs while overseeing staff, financial management, community partnerships, fundraising, facility operations, safety, and compliance. The Executive Director serves as the face of the Club, fostering relationships with families, community partners, donors, schools, and stakeholders to advance the organization's mission.

  


**Essential Responsibilities**


**Leadership \& Operations**


* Provide overall leadership and management of Club operations.
* Develop and implement strategic goals aligned with the organization's mission.
* Ensure a safe, welcoming, and inclusive environment for youth, families, staff, and volunteers.
* Maintain compliance with all licensing, organizational, and regulatory requirements.
* Monitor program quality, membership growth, attendance, and operational performance.


**Staff Leadership**


* Recruit, hire, train, supervise, coach, and evaluate staff.
* Foster a positive, collaborative, and accountable workplace culture.
* Support staff professional development and succession planning.
* Conduct regular staff meetings and performance evaluations.


**Program Management**


* Ensure the implementation of high\-quality youth development programming.
* Monitor program outcomes and continuously improve member experiences.
* Promote programs that support Academic Success, Healthy Lifestyles, Good Character \& Leadership, Workforce Readiness, STEM, Arts, and Sports \& Recreation.

  


**Financial Management**


* Develop and manage the Club budget.
* Monitor expenditures and ensure fiscal responsibility.
* Review financial reports and implement cost\-effective practices.
* Ensure compliance with grant and funding requirements.


**Community Engagement \& Partnerships**


* Build relationships with schools, businesses, community organizations, and government agencies.
* Represent the organization at community meetings and events.
* Develop partnerships that enhance programming and expand opportunities for youth.


**Resource Development**


* Support fundraising initiatives and donor stewardship.
* Assist with grant opportunities and sponsorship development.
* Participate in special events and community engagement efforts.


**Facility \& Safety**


* Oversee maintenance and cleanliness of the Club.
* Ensure adherence to all safety policies and emergency procedures.
* Maintain licensing standards and risk management practices.

**Minimum Qualifications**


* Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Nonprofit Management, Education, Human Services, Recreation, or a related field (Master's degree preferred).
* Five or more years of progressive leadership experience in nonprofit, education, youth development, recreation, or community programming.
* Minimum of three years of staff supervision and budget management experience.
* Experience managing multiple priorities and leading diverse teams.
* Strong knowledge of youth development principles and best practices.
* Valid driver's license and reliable transportation.


**Preferred Qualifications**


* Experience with Boys \& Girls Clubs or a similar youth\-serving organization.
* Experience managing grants and nonprofit funding.
* Experience with state licensing and regulatory compliance.
* Proven success in community engagement and partnership development.


**Knowledge, Skills \& Abilities**


* Strong leadership and decision\-making skills.
* Excellent verbal and written communication.
* Budgeting and financial management.
* Staff coaching and performance management.
* Conflict resolution and problem\-solving.
* Strategic planning and organizational development.
* Public speaking and relationship building.
* Proficiency with Microsoft Office and data management systems.


**Physical Requirements**


* Ability to move throughout the Club facility.
* Ability to lift up to 25 pounds.
* Occasional evening and weekend work required.
* Ability to travel between Club locations and community events as needed.

jobs.title: Executive Director: SculptureWalk
jobs.description:
Location: Sioux Falls, SD
  

Compensation: Competitive compensation based on experience.
  

Classification: Full\-time, salaried exempt
  

This role requires flexibility, including occasional evening and weekend work for events, donor
  

engagement, artist relations, board meetings, installation, de\-installation, and other mission critical activities
  

Reports to: Board of Directors
  

Supervises: N/A  



  

**Organizational Overview**  

SculptureWalk Sioux Falls is a 501(c)(3\) and is one of the largest annual exhibits of public
  

sculptures in the country. Our mission is to bring art to the people, and our guiding principle is to
  

be the highest quality, most professional, financially strong, artist friendly, year\-round outdoor
  

sculpture program in the United States. Curated by a blind jury of local dignitaries and art
  

enthusiasts, works are graciously loaned by the artists and installed in the spring through
  

downtown Sioux Falls, SD, to be continuously displayed all year.  



  

**Position Summary**  

The Executive Director (ED) is the chief executive and fundraiser for SculptureWalk Sioux Falls,
  

responsible for the organization’s leadership, financial sustainability, and long\-term success.
  

The ED is responsible for leading daily operations, advancing the strategic plan, achieving
  

organizational goals, and ensuring the organization has the resources, relationships, and
  

operating discipline needed to fulfill its mission.  

The ED maintains a strong working relationship with the Board of Directors through clear
  

communication, regular reporting, and appropriate respect for the distinction between board
  

governance and staff management.  



  

**First\-Year Priorities**  

* Build strong working relationships with the Board, artists, donors, sponsors, civic

partners, volunteers, and community stakeholders.
  

* Develop and execute a fundraising plan with clear revenue goals, donor stewardship

practices, sponsorship strategy, and grant opportunities.
  

* Assess current operations, staffing, volunteer needs, financial systems, artist processes,

and event logistics, then recommend improvements.
  

* Lead successful execution of the annual exhibit cycle, including artist communication,

site coordination, installation, de\-installation, maintenance, voting, sales, and related
  

community engagement.
  

* Create a year\-round engagement calendar that supports fundraising, public awareness,

donor cultivation, artist relationships, and community participation. **Essential Duties and Responsibilities**  

**Executive Leadership \& Strategy**  

* Serve as the organization’s chief executive, accountable for overall performance,

sustainability, and impact.
  

* Lead execution of the organization’s strategic plan and translate board\-approved

priorities into annual goals, operating plans, budgets, and measurable outcomes.
  

* Set organizational priorities and make final decisions on resource allocation and

strategic tradeoffs.
  

* Foster a mission\-driven, accountable, and collaborative culture.
* Lead day\-to\-day administrative and operational tasks.
  

**Financial Leadership \& Sustainability**  

* Manage the organization’s financial resources responsibly and in alignment with the

board\-approved budget, strategic priorities, donor restrictions, and nonprofit best
  

practices.
  

* Work with the finance firm, Treasurer, Finance Committee, and Board to support

budgeting, forecasting, financial reporting, audits or reviews, internal controls, and longterm financial planning.
  

* Provide timely, accurate, and transparent financial information to the Board, including

financial performance, risks, opportunities, and recommended corrective action when
  

needed.
  

* Ensure that organizational resources are used in a way that maximizes mission impact,

operational effectiveness, and long\-term sustainability.
  

* Manage funds, bank accounts, investments, insurance coverage, as well as invoicing

and payment collection. **Fundraising \& Resource Development**  

* Serve as the organization’s lead fundraiser, with primary staff responsibility for

developing, managing, and executing a comprehensive fundraising strategy across
  

individual giving, major gifts, sponsorships, grants, endowment, special campaigns, and
  

events.
  

* Cultivate, solicit, and steward donors, sponsors, foundations, artists, civic partners, and

institutional funders.
  

* Use Board members strategically to support fundraising through warm introductions,

relationship\-building, ambassadorship, and donor engagement, while maintaining staff
  

ownership of fundraising execution and follow\-through.  

  

**Board Relations \& Board Development**  

* Support the Board President, Executive Committee, and/or Nomination Committee in

board recruitment, orientation, training, succession planning, and governance
  

improvement efforts.
  

* Provide staff leadership, information, and tools that help the Board fulfill its governance,

fundraising, ambassadorship, and accountability responsibilities.
  

* Coordinate with Board President and Secretary to support effective board and

committee meetings, including agenda development, preparation of staff reports, and
  

follow\-up on assigned action items.  

  

**Community Engagement, Communications, \& External Relations**  

* Build and maintain strong relationships with artists, donors, sponsors, civic leaders,

downtown stakeholders, government partners, media, peer organizations, and
  

community members.
  

* Serve as the organization’s chief spokesperson and public representative.

**POSITION SPECIFICS**

Penn State is currently seeking a frontline fundraiser in the role of Assistant/Associate Director of Development to engage alumni and friends as partners in supporting Penn State Dickinson Law, one of the nation’s oldest law schools and a modern leader in antiracist legal education that produces profession\-ready lawyers with a commitment to service and the skills to excel in an increasingly global market. As a member of the Division of Development and Alumni Relations (DDAR), you can play an integral role in Penn State’s future and contribute to one of the most successful fundraising and alumni relations operations in the country.


Reporting to the Director of Development and Alumni Relations and working closely with the campus’ leadership, as well as other partners and central development offices, this Assistant/Associate Director of Development will:


* Identify and cultivate a prospect pool of graduates and other key constituencies;
* Articulate Penn State’s needs, ambitions, and values through excellent informal and formal communications, including written proposals;
* Plan and execute solicitation strategies leading to major gifts;
* Travel extensively throughout the region and across the country to pursue these strategies and build enduring relationships, tracked to a fixed and appropriately aggressive set of fundraising metrics.

The successful candidate will have:


* A track record of success in securing major gifts and meeting fundraising goals;
* Exemplary interpersonal and communication skills required to build relationships with sophisticated and high\-net worth audiences;
* The ability to create and maintain an inclusive and respectful environment for all students and colleagues;
* A proven ability to self\-motivate and work both independently and as part of a team;
* A commitment to professional development, learning, and being mentored.

DDAR is supportive of flexible work arrangements when aligned with the ability to meet the needs of the unit and the essential duties of the position. Questions related to flexible work arrangements should be directed to the hiring manager during the interview process.

**Penn State Dickinson Law**

Philanthropy will have a special impact for Penn State Dickinson Law, which draws strength from both its tradition of excellence in legal education having been established in 1834 in Carlisle, PA, and its current status as the primary location of a one law school, two location model for Penn State. The additional location for legal education is in University Park, PA. Both locations access the resources and opportunities of a world\-class research university. Its alumni include some of the nation’s finest attorneys, judges, government and corporate leaders, and legal scholars, and its core mission is to prepare students for 21st century profession\-readiness in the most marketable, cutting\-edge, and relevant areas of the law. At the same time, Penn State Dickinson Law cultivates its students’ awareness of the impact that they can have in the communities where they pursue their educations and ultimately build their careers. More information can be found at dickinsonlaw.psu.edu .

If you believe in the power of higher education—and philanthropy—to shape the public good, you’ll excel right here in Penn State’s Division of Development and Alumni Relations (DDAR). jobs.title: Executive Director of Development, Statler College
jobs.description:
**Job Summary**


The WVU Foundation’s mission is to enrich the lives of those touched by West Virginia University by maximizing charitable support and providing services to the University, its students, and affiliated organizations. The Foundation’s work is guided by core values of relationships, integrity, accountability, courage, innovation, and exceptional performance, ensuring that charitable contributions are used effectively to enhance student opportunities, faculty and staff resources, and institutional programs.


West Virginia University is distinctly a land grant, flagship and R1 institution where pride in our land\-grant mission runs deep, success is earned, and life\-changing experiences prepare Mountaineers to serve others and lead in West Virginia and the world.


The Benjamin M. Statler College of Engineering and Mineral Resources, established in 1887, is home to nearly 3,300 students. A culture of philanthropy and over 300 active partnerships with outside academic institutions, industry, national laboratory, state and federal agencies bolster both research production and student experience. These partnerships fuel career readiness, experiential learning, and award winning competition teams. Distinctions of excellence include:

* 1 of approximately 40 named engineering colleges in the United States.
* 1 of 156 accredited U.S. universities to offer a medical program.
* 1 of 5 universities with mining engineering and petroleum and natural gas engineering programs
* 1 of 4 colleges with an ABET\-accredited petroleum and natural gas engineering program
* 12 NSF Career Award winners since 2010
* 5 R\&D 100 Oscars of Innovation

The Senior/Executive Director of Development in the Benjamin M. Statler College of Engineering and Mineral Resources serves as the lead development officer for the College and provides strategic leadership for an advancement program that includes two gift officers. The embedded, onsite college leadership role reports directly to the WVU Foundation Associate Vice President, Schools and Units Development with dual reporting responsibilities to the College Dean.


**Duties and Responsibilities**

**Essential Functions and Responsibilities**

**Fundraising Leadership**

* Demonstrate the WVU Foundation core values of Trust, Transparency and Teamwork
* Manage all aspects of the Statler College development program, including managing a comprehensive major gift program to generate current gift revenue from individuals, corporations, and foundations, deferred gift revenue from individuals, annual giving programming and stewardship
* Serve as the Dean’s primary advisor on advancement matters
* Provide strategic guidance on the development and execution of major and principal gift development and solicitation to meet both donor and organizational priorities
* Supervise and mentor College development staff, including development of career and fundraising goals as appropriate.
* Manage a portfolio of major and principal gift donors and prospects, as defined by working with the WVU Foundation, through qualification, cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship
* Plan, schedule, and complete frequent travel, both regional and national, to contact prospects and donors in completion of portfolio/pipeline management
* Maintain a comprehensive working knowledge of the College and University, including mission, history, and current fundraising priorities assuring effective representation of all opportunities and priorities for private support

**Collaboration**

* Build and maintain effective relationships with College leadership, faculty, and staff to align advancement activities with program priorities. In addition to the dean, key relationships include department chairs, associate deans, career services, and marketing \& communications.
* Collaborate with Foundation leadership and central colleagues to develop strategies to link donor interest with college priorities
* Build and manage a network of relationships with prospective donors involving University and College leadership, faculty and volunteer to facilitate communication across entities and as well as with donors.
* Coordinating with Prospect Development and Strategy, suggest strategy meetings on multi\-interest prospects with fundraising colleagues to keep gift discussions moving forward in a donor centric fashion
* Coordinate with Donor Relations and appropriate fundraising colleagues to develop and execute personalized stewardship plans for select donors and prospects
* Coordinate with the Annual Fund team in the execution of a comprehensive annual fund strategy
* Collaborate with gift processing

**Organizational Management**

* Comply with all WVU Foundation reporting and accountability standards and coordinate development efforts with University Units development staff as needed
* Participate in the Foundation’s human resource functions as necessary, including conducting performance evaluations, developing promotion plans, resolving problems, and documenting information
* Participate in Foundation sponsored professional development, including but not limited to Plus Delta training, leadership development, and systems training.
* Complete additional assignments as may be required by the supervisor, other Foundation senior management or the College’s Dean as appropriate

**Impact in Your First Year**

**Within 3 months, you’ll:**

* Become familiar with the overarching structure of development at WVU Foundation and affiliated entities and the alignment between these organizations
* Attend onboarding, professional development and networking activities for newcomers
